Hi Jillian —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion I put together a concise phased-commit proposal so Acme can proceed with an internal approval path while preserving your cost predictability. Key points in the attached MAP-v3:

- Phase 1 (Pilot, 3 months): 50k tokens / mo baseline; Redwood provides $25k credit (one-time) applied against usage. No platform fee for pilot.
- Phase 2 (Ramp, month 4–9): 250k tokens / mo committed; 20% discount off list hosted rate; platform fee applies at a reduced rate (50% of list) starting month 4, waiver on overage for first two months of ramp.
- Phase 3 (Steady state, month 10+): f...

Priority summary (from our call):
- Target latency SLO: p95 < 120ms for short chat turns (~64 tokens)
- Long-form background generation: p95 < 800ms for 512-token responses
- Cost sensitivity: prefer tests that reflect tokens/sec and per-token cost at 95th percentile

Proposed focused A/B tests (we’ll run Redwood hosted vs Hyperscaler A API using the same model family):

Subject: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Team —

We just got the vendor compatibility report ...
text:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-08-12T09:14:00Z
Subject: Kite-XS variant: compatibility summary + ask for temporary pin

Team —

We just got the vendor compatibility report from Nimbus for the Kite-XS 4B family (see attached). High level: the model appears to run fine in our runtime, but there are two areas where the variant diverges from our standard tokenizer and KV layout which could cause downstream hallucination/regression for a subset of prompt shapes.

Quick summary: 
- Tokenization differences: subtle BOS/EOS treatment on edge-case punctuation sequences.
- KV cache ordering: variant uses packed KV dims which changes memory layout assumptions in a few lower-level kernels.

Ask: before we let this variant into the public catalog, I want ...

Ava —

text:
From: Laura Mendez <laura.mendez@havenrx.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Mike Johnson <mike.johnson@havenrx.com>, sre@havenrx.com
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2026 08:12:04 -0700
Subject: Production: sharp latency + error spike after model update

Ava —

We're seeing a serious degradation in production since the model upgrade rolled on Friday night (redwood model v2.3 with the new quantization profile). Key impacts:
- p50 unchanged, but p99 latency jumped from ~420ms to ~1.8s over the weekend
- error rate (5xx) rose to ~4.8% on certain heavy-traffic endpoints
- throughput dropped ~22% under sustained load; timeouts reported by our downstream processing

This is live traffic for our med-prioritization flow — it's directly impacting response SLAs and our internal handling. We need an immediate plan: rollback, mitigation, or hotfix. Our SRE can open a call if helpful.

Please treat as ...

text:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Sofia Garcia <sofia@leanretail.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-05-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick fit questions after your self-signup

Hi Sofia — thanks for signing up and trying Redwood! I pulled your account and saw some early activity — would love to do a brief call to make sure a demo is a fit and to surface the right pilot advice. Before we schedule, two quick areas to help us tailor the session: 

1) Primary use case: what are you most looking to build? (examples: live customer support summarization, search/embeddings, content generation for marketing, internal knowledge base)
2) Traffic: rough QPS or daily requests today and target after 3 months (ballpark is fine)
3) Timeline & priority: are you looking for a pilot in 2 weeks / 1 month / 2+ months?

If you can send 2–3 short bullets on those, I’ll propose three demo slots ...

Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: 

- G...
text:
From: Claire Huang <claire@findly.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@findly.com>
Date: Tue, 12 May 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Relevancy pilot — notes + data ask

Ava / team — thanks for the demo this morning. Quick recap from our side and a few asks so your engs can scope the pilot: 

- Goal: replace heuristic reordering in our search results with a lightweight reranker for top 50 candidates; target improvement: +10–15% NDCG@10 and measurable reduction in user re-clicks.
- Data: we can provide ~2.5M query -> candidate lists (JSONL) with click labels for a rolling 90d window. Average candidates per query ~120, median 48.
- Security: we need an overview of how Redwood handles PII in logs, where embeddings are stored, and whether keys can be scoped to a VPC/private deployment.
- Integration asks (high level): SDK vs HTTP direct; auth options; support for struc...

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. As we finalize the MAP for the POC close, the platform team asked for a short playbook that defines the ramp/pacing profiles we'll run and the pass/fail gating for TTFT, sustained throughput, and queue length under load. They want to understand how Redwood will validate burst vs steady-state behavior and any recommended rate-limit tuning to avoid head-of-line blocking.

Can you share a proposed schedule (dates/times), the exact ramp scripts you plan to run, and the acceptance thresholds your team will use? We need this to commit the final sign-off on 5/12.

TIA — Emma

From: Samir Gupta <samir@launchpad.co>
To: Ava Chen <ava_chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Elena Morales <elena@foundrylabs.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Apr 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot support request: NovaForge evaluation and trial extension

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the quick product demo last week — the team at NovaForge (we're an accelerator cohort company) is excited about Redwood's latency and cost controls. We're preparing a 6-week evaluation across two internal apps and would like to request: 1) a 60-day trial extension on the hosted plan, and 2) a $10,000 promotional credit to cover pilot usage and a small production-like load.

High level:
- Goal: validate inference cost at 2k TPS over short burst loads, evaluate model variants, and run our prompt-eval suite.
- Timeline: start in early May; run for ~6 weeks.
- Key success metrics: < 250ms median latency and < $0.02/token average cost for ...
